assets.abstract: Consciousness is commonly considered to be a single entity, as expressed in the term "unity of consciousness", and neurobiologists are fond of believing that, sooner or later, they will be able to determine its neural correlate (rather than its neural correlates). Here I propose an alternative view, derived from compelling experimental and clinical studies of the primate visual cortex, which suggest that consciousness is not a single unity but consists instead of many components (the micro-consc

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Visionary cosmologies — self-transcendence, mystical experience, numinous, awe, cosmic consciousness.
